methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $150M - $29.0B.

data.narrative:
Tytan Technologies, a Series A-stage defense and security company founded by Balazs Nagy and Batuhan Yumurtaci, is assessed against a composite universe of 10 peers (2 public, 8 private) operating in the Defense & Security sector. The peer-median approach anchors this analysis, drawing on four same-stage, same-sector comparables: Scale AI ($29.0B), Stark Defence ($500M), Vatn Systems ($300M), and Ares Industries ($150M).

The peer-median valuation method yields $400M, with the sample range spanning $150M to $29.0B. The wide dispersion reflects heterogeneity within early-stage defense tech—Scale AI's outlier valuation likely reflects either exceptional traction or distinct positioning relative to Tytan's core application. The median of $400M is therefore more representative of typical Series A capitalization in this sector than either tail observation.

Analysis supports a valuation range of $340M to $460M, with $400M as the central estimate. Tytan has raised €46M to date. Key uncertainties include company-specific technical differentiation (not provided in available data), customer traction metrics, and whether peer valuations reflect comparable revenue or deployment stage. The peer-median method, while applicable given sample size and stage homogeneity, remains the sole triangulation available. Validation would benefit from revenue multiples or customer concentration data to refine positioning within the derived range.
